message.content: {"items":[{"title":"European Working Conditions Survey 2021 - Germany","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14003","justification":"Contains detailed information on working conditions, health, work-related stress, and occupational risks in Germany in the 2020s; relevant to working conditions and health-related aspects of specific conditions."},{"title":"German Health Update (GEDA 2021)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.21241/ssoar.88979","justification":"National health survey for Germany with data on specific diseases, health status, health care use and policies in the early 2020s; focuses on health care services and medical conditions."},{"title":"EU-SILC 2020 – Germany (European Union Statistics on Income and Living Conditions)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13726","justification":"German component of EU-SILC includes modules on health, limitations due to health problems, and access to health care; useful for studying health conditions and health care services in Germany in the 2020s."},{"title":"EU-SILC 2021 – Germany (European Union Statistics on Income and Living Conditions)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13904","justification":"Continuation of EU-SILC for Germany with 2021 data, including health status, chronic conditions, and health care access; relevant to specific diseases and health policies in the 2020s."},{"title":"German General Social Survey – ALLBUS 2021","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13939","justification":"National social survey covering attitudes towards health, social inequality, and sometimes health care; provides context on perceptions of health services and working life in Germany in the 2020s."},{"title":"ISSP 2021 – Health and Health Care: German National Dataset","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/en/study/ZA7601","justification":"International Social Survey Programme module focused specifically on health and health care with a German sample; directly addresses health care services, policies, and perceptions in the 2020s."},{"title":"Politbarometer 2020 (Germany) – Cumulative 2020 File","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13721","justification":"Regular political opinion survey including items on health policy, COVID-19, and health care services in Germany; relevant to health care services and policies and societal discussion of disease and health."},{"title":"Politbarometer 2021 (Germany) – Cumulative 2021 File","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13863","justification":"Captures public opinion in Germany in 2021 with strong focus on COVID-19, health care policies, and related issues; useful for examining health policies and public perception in the 2020s."},{"title":"Special Eurobarometer 501: Attitudes towards the impact of digitisation and automation on daily life – Germany dataset","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13741","justification":"Includes German subsample with questions on digital media use, work, and perceived impact on quality of life and possibly health; links media/communication with working conditions and well-being in the 2020s."},{"title":"German Family Panel (pairfam), 14th wave (2021/22)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/pairfam.5678.14.0.0","justification":"Longitudinal survey including German respondents with detailed questions on partnership, family, mental and physical health, and sometimes work-life balance; relevant to specific conditions, health care use, and working conditions in the 2020s."}]}
